name: Swordfish Steaks with Mango and Avocado Salsa (Weir)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 fresh jalapeno chili pepper, seeded and minced
1/4 cup chopped fresh cilantro, plus cilantro sprigs for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off the flesh from each side of the big, flat pit in one mango to form two large pieces. Discard the pit.
2. Score the flesh lengthwise and then crosswise into 1/2-inch squares, cutting through to the skin.
3. Holding the mango over a bowl, slip the blade between the skin and flesh to cut away the cubes. Add them to a bowl.
4. Add the avocado, jalapeno, onion, lime zest, lime juice, orange juice, chopped cilantro and 1 tablespoon olive oil. Mix well and season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
5. In one or two large frying pans, warm 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium-high heat.
6. Add the swordfish steaks and cook until lightly golden on one side, about 5 minutes. Turn the fish and continue cooking until lightly golden on the second side and opaque throughout when cut with a knife, about 5 more minutes.
7. Place the swordfish steaks on warmed plates and top with the salsa. Garnish with lime wedges and cilantro sprigs. Serve 6.
